phases.name: Reconnaissance
phases.description: Adversary gathers information to plan future operations.

techniques.name: Active Scanning
techniques.description: Probe victim infrastructure via network scans (port, vuln, wordlist).

techniques.name: Gather Victim Host Information
techniques.description: Collect host configuration, software, firmware, client details.

techniques.name: Gather Victim Identity Information
techniques.description: Collect emails, employee names, credentials for targeting.

techniques.name: Search Open Technical Databases
techniques.description: Query WHOIS, DNS, certificate, scan, and CDN databases.

techniques.name: Search Open Websites/Domains
techniques.description: Mine social media, search engines, code repos for intel.

phases.name: Resource Development
phases.description: Adversary establishes infrastructure, capabilities, and accounts to support operations.

techniques.name: Acquire Infrastructure
techniques.description: Buy or lease servers, domains, DNS, botnets for staging/C2.

techniques.name: Compromise Accounts
techniques.description: Take over existing accounts (social, email, cloud) for operations.

techniques.name: Develop Capabilities
techniques.description: Build custom malware, exploits, code-signing certs in-house.

techniques.name: Obtain Capabilities
techniques.description: Buy or steal tooling, exploits, certificates from third parties.

techniques.name: Establish Accounts
techniques.description: Create new identities (email, social, cloud) to support targeting.

phases.name: Initial Access
phases.description: Adversary gains a foothold in the target environment.

techniques.name: Phishing
techniques.description: Spearphishing attachment/link/service to deliver payload or harvest credentials.

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Weaponize vulnerability in an internet-exposed app (web, VPN, mail).

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Use compromised or default credentials (local, domain, cloud) to log in.

techniques.name: External Remote Services
techniques.description: Abuse VPN, RDP, SSH, Citrix exposed externally for persistent access.

techniques.name: Supply Chain Compromise
techniques.description: Tamper with software, hardware, or dependencies before delivery.

phases.name: Execution
phases.description: Adversary runs malicious code on a local or remote system.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
techniques.description: Execute via bash, PowerShell, cmd, Python, JavaScript, etc.

techniques.name: User Execution
techniques.description: Rely on user to open a malicious link, attachment, or executable.

techniques.name: Scheduled Task/Job
techniques.description: Abuse cron, at, systemd timers, Windows Task Scheduler to run code.

techniques.name: System Services
techniques.description: Start or modify system services (sc, systemctl) to execute payloads.

techniques.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
techniques.description: Use WMI to execute commands locally or remotely.

phases.name: Persistence
phases.description: Adversary maintains access across reboots, credential changes, and interruptions.

techniques.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
techniques.description: Run key, Startup folder, LaunchAgent, systemd unit at boot/login.

techniques.name: Create Account
techniques.description: Create local, domain, or cloud accounts for future access.

techniques.name: Account Manipulation
techniques.description: Modify credentials, keys, MFA, roles on an existing account.

techniques.name: Server Software Component
techniques.description: Install web shell, IIS module, transport agent, or SQL proc.

techniques.name: Create or Modify System Process
techniques.description: Install service, launchd, systemd unit to persist at process level.

phases.name: Privilege Escalation
phases.description: Adversary obtains higher-level permissions on a system or network.

techniques.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
techniques.description: Exploit kernel/service bug to elevate from user to SYSTEM/root.

techniques.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
techniques.description: Bypass UAC, sudo, setuid, elevated token APIs.

techniques.name: Process Injection
techniques.description: Inject DLL/shellcode into another process to gain its privileges.

techniques.name: Domain Policy Modification
techniques.description: Modify GPO, trust, or federation config for escalated access.

techniques.name: Access Token Manipulation
techniques.description: Impersonate or duplicate tokens to run with another user's rights.

techniques.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
techniques.description: Encode, encrypt, pack, or steganographically hide code/data.

techniques.name: Indicator Removal
techniques.description: Clear event logs, shell history, artifacts on host.

techniques.name: System Binary Proxy Execution
techniques.description: Use LOLBins (rundll32, mshta, regsvr32) to run code signed.

techniques.name: Impair Defenses
techniques.description: Disable AV, EDR, firewalls, logging, MFA enforcement.

techniques.name: Masquerading
techniques.description: Rename files, spoof signatures, mimic legit task/service names.

phases.name: Credential Access
phases.description: Adversary steals account names, passwords, tokens, or keys.

techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: Guess passwords via spray, credential stuffing, or offline cracking.

techniques.name: OS Credential Dumping
techniques.description: Dump LSASS, SAM, /etc/shadow, DCSync, NTDS.dit.

techniques.name: Credentials from Password Stores
techniques.description: Extract secrets from browsers, keychain, vaults, credential managers.

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials
techniques.description: Find creds in files, registry, env vars, CI logs, bash history.

techniques.name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets
techniques.description: Kerberoast, AS-REP roast, Golden/Silver Ticket, pass-the-ticket.

techniques.name: Account Discovery
techniques.description: Enumerate local, domain, cloud, email accounts.

techniques.name: System Information Discovery
techniques.description: Gather OS, arch, hostname, patch, hardware details.

techniques.name: File and Directory Discovery
techniques.description: Browse filesystem for sensitive files, shares, configs.

techniques.name: Network Service Discovery
techniques.description: Port-scan internal hosts for services and versions.

techniques.name: Remote System Discovery
techniques.description: List other hosts via AD, ARP, DNS, hosts file, SMB.

phases.name: Lateral Movement
phases.description: Adversary moves through the environment to reach objectives.

techniques.name: Remote Services
techniques.description: Use RDP, SSH, SMB, WinRM, VNC with valid creds to pivot.

techniques.name: Exploitation of Remote Services
techniques.description: Exploit unpatched service (SMB, RPC, web) to move laterally.

techniques.name: Internal Spearphishing
techniques.description: Phish internal users from a compromised internal mailbox.

techniques.name: Lateral Tool Transfer
techniques.description: Copy tools between internal hosts via SMB, SCP, built-in utils.

techniques.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material
techniques.description: Pass-the-hash, pass-the-ticket, app tokens, cookies.

phases.name: Collection
phases.description: Adversary gathers data of interest to the objective.

techniques.name: Archive Collected Data
techniques.description: Compress/encrypt data (zip, rar, 7z) before exfil.

techniques.name: Data from Local System
techniques.description: Enumerate and stage files from local filesystem.

techniques.name: Email Collection
techniques.description: Harvest mailboxes via PST, IMAP, EWS, Graph API.

techniques.name: Screen Capture
techniques.description: Capture desktop screenshots periodically or on demand.

techniques.name: Input Capture
techniques.description: Keylogger, credential prompt, GUI hook to capture user input.

phases.name: Command and Control
phases.description: Adversary communicates with compromised systems to control them.

techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol
techniques.description: C2 over HTTP(S), DNS, SMTP, mail, or messaging protocols.

techniques.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
techniques.description: Download additional tools from C2 onto the victim.

techniques.name: Encrypted Channel
techniques.description: Wrap C2 traffic in TLS or custom symmetric/asymmetric crypto.

techniques.name: Proxy
techniques.description: Use internal/external proxies or redirectors to hide origin.

techniques.name: Protocol Tunneling
techniques.description: Tunnel C2 inside another protocol (DNS, ICMP, SSH).

phases.name: Exfiltration
phases.description: Adversary steals data from the target environment.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
techniques.description: Send data back through the existing C2 channel.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
techniques.description: Use a different protocol (DNS, FTP, SMTP) than C2.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
techniques.description: Upload to Dropbox, S3, Telegram, paste sites, Gist.

techniques.name: Automated Exfiltration
techniques.description: Automated collection+upload scripts on schedule or triggers.

techniques.name: Scheduled Transfer
techniques.description: Exfil data at fixed times to blend into normal traffic.

phases.name: Impact
phases.description: Adversary manipulates, interrupts, or destroys systems and data.

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
techniques.description: Ransomware: encrypt files, demand ransom.

techniques.name: Data Destruction
techniques.description: Wipe files, overwrite disks, drop tables.

techniques.name: Inhibit System Recovery
techniques.description: Delete shadow copies, backups, disable recovery.

techniques.name: Network Denial of Service
techniques.description: Flood network to deny service (volumetric DDoS).

techniques.name: Endpoint Denial of Service
techniques.description: Exhaust endpoint resources (CPU, memory, sockets).

techniques.name: Account Access Removal
techniques.description: Lock out users by changing or deleting credentials.
